widgets_values: Half-body portrait of a yellow eyed, busty, beautiful young woman in her twenties, with blonde hair, red lips, wearing a tight white top. Her hair is styled in loose waves. She is on the beach, the shore is on the left and mountains to the right.

Muting the Loaders and Switching to the Global Model to remain VRAM friendly, but if You have the VRAM Budget, You can go crazy and enable these, Mix Any SD Family Models and LoRA Setup. There is No Limit, You Can add a setup Like This to Each and Every Tile, Seams and Intersections, or Re-Use certain setups for specific Tiles with Retargeting.
Be aware: Vastly Different Models / Settings can and Will result in noticable Seam Issues.

## Usage

Load Your **Image** and **Models**

In the **LoRA Loader**, **enable** and set up Your **LoRA**s if any (first number column is **Model Strenght**, second is **Clip**). Tip: **Click** the **Arrow** buttons for **fine** adjustments, **CTRL** + **Click** for **bigger** steps. Click the **Number** to **Reset**.

Use the **Prompt Library** to load Your **preset** or write **Prompt** and **Negative** prompt

Adjust **Guider**, **Sampler**, **Scheduler**

If You **don't** want to upscale the **Image**, use the **Set Mode** nodes to **bypass** both **Upscale Image (using Model)** and **Upscale Image** nodes.

If Your **Upscale Model** is let's say **4x** but You only want **2x**, You can Resize **after** Upscaling: Make sure **both** Upscale Image (using Model) and Upscale Image nodes set to **Enabled**. Set the **Resolution Calulator**'s **Multiplier** to **2** which will take the **Input Image Dimensions**, multiplies it by **2** and passes it to the Upscale Image **Width** and **Height** parammeters.

Finally, set the self-explanatory parameters of the **Tiled Sampler** and **run** the **Workflow**.

---

## Important!

---

Do **NOT** use **Mute** on Upscaler / Resizer! Enable or Bypass only.

---

The **Multiplier** is applied to the **original** Input Image dimensions. With **Multiplier** set to **2**, You'll double the original image size regardless of what Upscale Model You're using if Resizer is enabled. When the Resizer is Bypassed, the Multiplier does nothing.

---

## Color Codes

### Red Nodes

Normally **Loaders** and things You **need** to change for Your specific setup **before** First Use

---

### Purple Nodes

Things You probably **will** interact with the most

---

### Blue Nodes

Generally just **stet it and forget it** nodes that You'll set once

---

### Black Nodes
You don't ever really need to touch those
Portrait of a yellow eyed beautiful young woman in her twenties, red lips, wearing a tight white top. Her hair is styled in loose waves. She is on the beach, the water is clear and turquoise.
Portrait of a yellow eyed beautiful young woman in her twenties, red lips, dressed in a tight white top. Green mountains in the background.
Chest and shoulder of a busty young woman wearing a tight white top. Sandy ground and foothills of distant mountains in the bacground.
Chest of a busty young woman wearing a tight white top. She is on the beach, the water is clear and turquoise.

It is not necessary to make all these connections, this is just for demonstration. You could use just the Guiders for example to prompt each region. Or using the retargeting rules, You could re-use Guiders, Samplers and Sigmas on multiple tiles as it fits your specific case. And this is not the limit either. You can add more (or less) if You run a 1x2, 1x3, 3x3 or any variation grid for example, and You can add even more for seams and intersections, too. Keep in mind that Traversal Mode changes Execution Order.

s = sqrt((N2 * 1000000) / (N0 * N1))

out_1 (i, label = Width) = round((N0 * s) / 64) * 64
out_2 (i, label = Height) = round((N1 * s) / 64) * 64
